directive.title: RFQ Authorization Observability — Denial Rate Dashboard Metric

auditor.summary: The build has fundamental issues that prevent it from working correctly. The most critical problem is a schema mismatch: the authorization_audit_log table has no 'outcome' column, but all queries filter on `action = 'DENY'` as if there were an allow/deny outcome. The actual schema only has an 'action' column (text) that stores action types. Additionally, the dashboard page and sparkline component use incorrect prop signatures for existing components (KPI, AlertCard, ResponsiveBarChart), which will cause TypeScript compilation failures. The METRICS_READ_KEY security check is also implemented incorrectly — it's optional rather than mandatory. The build cannot be shipped in its current state.

top_issues.description: Schema mismatch: The authorization_audit_log table has no 'outcome' column. The spec mentions outcome ENUM('allow','deny') but the actual schema only has 'action' column (text). The queries filter on `action = 'DENY'` but the action column stores action types like 'read', 'write', not allow/deny outcomes. This will cause all queries to return zero results or incorrect data.

KPI component usage has incorrect props. The actual KPI component accepts { label, value, sub, badge, accent, className } but the dashboard page passes { title, trend, trendDirection } which don't exist on the component. This will cause TypeScript compilation errors.

AlertCard component usage has incorrect props. The actual AlertCard accepts { severity, title, detail, action?, aside? } but the dashboard page passes { title, description, severity, timestamp }. 'description' should be 'detail', and 'timestamp' is not a valid prop.
